DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-24-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

James D. Banks; 415-587-5334; plaintiff in pro per, appearing in person on their own behalf.
Raymond M. Yetka; Utrecht & Lenvin, LLP; 415-357-0600; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendants Courthouse Ventures, Inc., Joseph J. Giraudo, et al.

Contested. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES BANKS' DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. The cause of action for services rendered, as stated in opposition is based on the theory of quantum meruit. Such cause of action requires factual support that "the services were rendered under some understanding or expectation of both parties that compensation therefor was to be made." Cross-complaint currently appears to allege that in exchange for exclusive possession of the premises Banks requested and Courthouse Ventures provided services for which reasonable rental value is being sought. No other services are alleged. In the context of the quantum meruit claim this appears to be an allegation that Banks had exclusive possession and it was the understanding of both parties that compensation would be rendered in exchange. Is Court House Ventures alleging some kind of a rental agreement between the parties? Courthouse Ventures shall have a leave to amend to allege what services were rendered to Banks and what was the understanding/expectation of both parties regarding compensation. If Courthouse Ventures is in fact alleging some form of implied rental agreement (combination of current allegations in the complaint and reference to quantum meruit theory in opposition at 3-17) Courthouse Ventures shall so allege. Courthouse Ventures shall also only allege a claim for damages for reasonable rental value that is not already made in the pending unlawful detainer in which reasonable rental value is sought for a period from February 27, 2017 to entry of judgment.
The cause of action for quiet title appears to be incomplete. Paragraph 18 stops in mid-sentence suggesting that Courthouse Ventures intended to include the date of determination of quiet title. Courthouse Ventures shall have leave to clarify. Furthermore, Courthouse Ventures claims to be an equitable owner of the property and asserts that Banks claims and interest adverse to Courthouse Ventures. The Court of Appeals in its September 11, 2020 Order in this action stated: "A quiet title cause of action generally has two elements: (1) "the plaintiff is the owner and in possession of the land," and (2) "the defendant claims an interest therein adverse to [the plaintiff]." Courthouse Ventures shall have leave to amend to allege facts to support both elements or to allege facts supporting any exception to the general rule.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
